theme: Nova enterprise client go-live crisis plus Vertex demo risk and Atlas contract blocker

events.text: Enterprise client Arcwave went live at 09:00 — onboarding pipeline throwing 503s on first user batch of 200
REVERT: onboarding-pipeline.py — tenant provisioning failing on multi-org payload
NOVA-101 status Critical — onboarding blocked for Arcwave, 200 users pending provisioning
URGENT: Arcwave go-live incident bridge — CTO called at 09:01, all engineering leads joined
Infra: tenant DB provisioning step timing out after 8s — timeout threshold set to 5s in config, causing all failures
fix: raise DB provisioning timeout threshold from 5s to 15s in infra config
NOVA-101 escalated — SLA breach window opens in 45 minutes if not resolved
Priya (CSM): Arcwave account manager Marcus calling — asking for written ETA within 10 minutes or escalating to their CTO
Subject: Arcwave go-live failure — response required | Body: Onboarding broken at launch, Marcus expects written ETA by 09:15 or escalates to their CTO
test: simulate multi-org tenant provisioning under 200-user concurrent load
Auth service cold start latency spike — 4.2s p95 in production after last night's deploy, stakeholder demo at 11:00
VTX-55 status At Risk — auth latency 4.2s p95 is 3x above acceptable threshold for demo
fix: pre-warm auth service connection pool on startup to eliminate cold start delay
QA: login flow passing locally but flaky in staging — 3 failures in last 10 runs, demo in 2 hours
VTX-57 dependency risk flagged — demo environment not validated, fallback plan needed
test: auth cold-start under 50-concurrent-session spike mimicking demo load
Finance approval for revised contract still pending — day 5 with no ETA, legal team citing backlog
ATL-210 status Waiting Finance Approval — 5 days stalled, contract sign-off blocking revenue milestone
feat: add batch processing to analytics export pipeline — reduces export time by 65% on large datasets
Sprint velocity at 112% — analytics batch feature completed 2 days ahead of schedule, no blockers
Hotfix deployed — provisioning timeout raised to 15s, retrying Arcwave user batch now
REVERT: onboarding-pipeline.py — hotfix introduced duplicate org-id assignment bug on concurrent provisioning
NOVA-101 reverted to Blocked — provisioning hotfix caused org-id collision for 12 accounts
Engineering: reverting hotfix — 12 accounts have duplicate tenant IDs, manual cleanup required before retry
Subject: Arcwave onboarding status update | Body: First fix failed, second fix deploying now, revised ETA 09:40 — 30 minutes from now
fix: add idempotency check before org-id assignment — prevents collision on concurrent provisioning
ATL-212 scope review opened — client raised reporting dashboard requirement post-sign-off
PM: client wants reporting dashboard added to current sprint — not in original spec, estimates needed today
merge: optimize reporting query index — response time reduced 38% on 500K row datasets
Team standup: 6 PRs queued for review, all green, no blockers — Orion sprint in excellent health
Arcwave: 23 of 200 users provisioned — pipeline still unstable, 8 retries in last 5 minutes
NOVA-101 update — 23 of 200 users provisioned, retry count 8, incident duration 15 minutes
test: validate idempotency fix under 200-user concurrent provisioning load on staging
CTO: written status update every 10 minutes until resolved — SLA breach in 30 minutes
Demo prep sync added — 30 minutes before 11:00 stakeholder demo, engineering lead hosting
VTX-58 flagged — fallback demo environment on backup cluster needs validation by 10:00
PM: stakeholder demo cannot be postponed — product VP and 2 enterprise prospects attending at 11:00
fix: increase auth session token refresh window from 10m to 20m — reduces mid-session expiry risk
Client escalating dashboard request — pushing to add it to current sprint, PM escalating to product leadership
Subject: Reporting dashboard scope request | Body: Client formally requesting dashboard addition in current delivery window — decision needed today
Provisioning pipeline stabilized — 89 of 200 Arcwave users now onboarded, no new errors
perf: reduce memory footprint in notification service by 22% — eliminates OOM risk under peak load
ORN-80 Done — notification performance milestone delivered 2 days ahead of sprint end
QA confirmed zero regressions after performance patch — staging merge approved
4 accounts still failing provisioning — DB schema mismatch on legacy org_type field
fix: handle legacy org_type schema variant in provisioning validator — covers pre-2023 account format
NOVA-103 opened — legacy schema edge case not covered in migration script, 4 accounts affected
PM and product in emergency sync — reporting dashboard estimate being drafted, feasibility under review
ATL-213 opened — reporting dashboard feasibility and sprint impact under review
docs: draft scope amendment for reporting dashboard — effort estimate and delivery timeline
Auth latency down to 1.8s p95 after connection pool pre-warm fix — demo environment stabilizing
VTX-55 updated to In Progress — auth fix validated in staging, p95 now 1.8s from 4.2s
test: simulate 50-user concurrent login spike — validating p95 stays below 2s threshold
QA: 3 consecutive clean login flows in staging — cautiously green, continuing monitoring
All 200 Arcwave users provisioned — incident duration 27 minutes, SLA breach confirmed at 20-minute threshold
NOVA-101 Resolved — all 200 users provisioned, post-mortem scheduled 14:00 today
Subject: Arcwave onboarding complete | Body: All 200 users provisioned at 09:27 — 27-minute incident, SLA breach to be reviewed, post-mortem at 14:00
Priya (CSM): Marcus (Arcwave) acknowledged resolution — flagged frustration with launch failure, relationship needs recovery plan
merge: analytics dashboard cache layer — 40% reduction in dashboard load time for reports over 100K rows
Leadership acknowledged sprint performance — analytics batch and cache milestones both delivered ahead of schedule
Reporting dashboard estimate complete: 6-8 engineering days — outside current sprint, cannot fit without pushing existing deliverables
ATL-214 flagged Watch — scope expansion requires leadership decision, client expecting answer today
Subject: Reporting dashboard timeline | Body: 6-8 days engineering effort — PM recommends next sprint, client VP response needed today
Client unhappy with dashboard deferral — PM managing expectations, escalating to client VP
refactor: session token handler — remove redundant DB lookup on token refresh, reduces latency 15%
VTX-60 In Progress — demo environment full validation starting
Demo deck updated — auth flow showing corrected response times, narrative aligned with product VP
docs: sprint velocity summary — 112% velocity, 2 performance milestones, 5 features shipped
ORN-82 Done — analytics export batch feature delivered ahead of sprint end
Internal QA review complete — no open issues, all Orion sprint deliverables production-ready
Post-incident monitoring active — provisioning pipeline health 100%, all 200 Arcwave accounts confirmed active
NOVA-104 opened — improve provisioning retry logic with exponential backoff to prevent future timeout failures
docs: post-incident remediation checklist — 5 action items for provisioning pipeline hardening
Finance approval still pending — PM flagging to CTO, 5-day delay now blocking contract sign-off and revenue recognition
ATL-210 escalated to executive visibility — 5-day finance approval stall blocking revenue milestone
Subject: Finance approval delay — executive action required | Body: Contract blocked 5 days, revenue milestone at risk if not resolved by EOD
PM confirmed demo narrative rehearsed — auth stable, product VP aligned, confidence high for 11:00
fix: final auth token edge case — handle expired token during concurrent session create
Sprint KPIs all green — velocity 112%, zero defects shipped, 2 performance milestones ahead of schedule
perf: final query optimization — reduces p99 latency from 480ms to 290ms on analytics aggregation
Arcwave CSM requesting SLA credit review — 27-minute incident exceeded 20-minute SLA threshold by 7 minutes
NOVA-105 opened — SLA breach assessment for Arcwave, 27-minute incident vs 20-minute SLA
Subject: SLA review — Arcwave go-live | Body: 27-minute incident exceeded 20-minute SLA — credit discussion and exec relationship call recommended
CTO: post-mortem expanded — SLA clause review, root cause, and Arcwave relationship recovery all on agenda
ATL-215 waiting client VP decision on dashboard scope — response expected by EOD
Client escalating dashboard decision to internal VP — response committed by 17:00 today
merge: analytics export batch improvement — ships to production tonight with zero risk items open
ORN-84 closed — batch export feature production-ready
Demo environment final check — all 8 critical flows passing, auth p95 stable at 1.6s
11:00 stakeholder demo confirmed — product VP, 2 enterprise prospects, and engineering lead attending
Provisioning retry improvement scoped — exponential backoff will reduce timeout failure rate by 60% per load test
feat: implement adaptive retry with exponential backoff in tenant provisioning pipeline
NOVA-104 In Progress — exponential backoff retry improvement underway
PM risk flag: if dashboard deferred AND finance approval delayed beyond today, delivery milestone slips by full week
Subject: Dual blocker risk — Atlas delivery | Body: Finance approval (5 days) and dashboard scope decision both unresolved — milestone at risk if both not cleared today
ATL-216 risk summary — two open blockers, both require resolution today to protect delivery
QA sign-off on demo environment — all flows passing, auth stable, fallback cluster ready
docs: demo run-of-show updated — 8-flow narrative with timing, fallback steps if auth degrades
Consistent delivery this sprint — 5 features merged, 2 performance milestones, velocity 112%
refactor: simplify event tracking handler — removes 340 lines of dead code, no behaviour change
Root cause confirmed — provisioning timeout config was not updated after infra migration 3 weeks ago
NOVA-106 post-mortem action — audit all infra config values after every future migration as mandatory step
docs: post-mortem draft — root cause (stale timeout config), remediation (idempotency fix), prevention (config audit gate)
Finance team responding — approval expected by 12:00 today
ATL-210 updated — finance approval ETA 12:00, one blocker potentially clearing today
Demo walkthrough run complete — product VP aligned on narrative, team confident for 11:00
VTX-60 closed — demo environment fully validated, fallback cluster confirmed ready
test: final regression pass — all 34 demo-path scenarios passing
Analytics pipeline optimization shipped — export now 3x faster on datasets over 1M rows
ORN-85 closed — sprint in best health this quarter, all deliverables production-ready
Marcus (Arcwave) confirmed open to relationship recovery call — pending SLA credit outcome
Subject: Arcwave relationship recovery | Body: Marcus open to exec call this week — recommend scheduling within 48 hours before sentiment hardens
NOVA-107 opened — exec relationship recovery call to be scheduled with Arcwave within 48 hours
PM: if finance approves by noon and client VP accepts dashboard deferral, Atlas delivery back on track for original date
docs: conditional delivery timeline — two paths depending on today's blocker resolutions
ATL-217 monitoring — both blockers expected to resolve today, PM watching outcomes
Team aligned and confident — demo fully prepared, auth stable at p95 1.6s, QA signed off
docs: sprint performance report — velocity 112%, features delivered, milestones achieved
Best sprint delivery this quarter — team morale high, no open risks heading into next sprint
